notes: Works after compiling a custom Wine version to use. Once in, the game runs alright. I get an error for "Unsupported video card detected!", but the game loads fine. Overall performance is pretty low on my machine. Compared to Windows I've had to lower every video setting to get playable FPS.
title: MechWarrior Online™ Solaris 7

notes: Complains about "Unsupported GPU" on start. Was able to login the first time, but then got an "Unexpected error" and crash. Second attempt (again with "Unsupported GPU") plays the intro videos, then "Unexpected error" with black screen and mouse cursor.
title: MechWarrior Online™ Solaris 7

notes: Required a hack to static ULONG get_full_path_helper() in .../dlls/ntdll/path.c, which is itself a hacky mess in need of fixing.  DX11 and Vulkan support are pretty crap right now, even with dxvk and vulkan dlls installed in the prefix (via winetricks).  DX9 perf also pretty bad, but better than DX11.
title: MechWarrior Online™ Solaris 7

notes: Works only after wine patches. Proton haven`t it. Simpliest way: Install lutris - install wine tkg-mwo-4.1 - import game from steam - use custom runner - tkg-mwo-4.1 - set paths in options for prefixes and .exe ( for exe: /home/user/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/MechWarrior Online/Bin64/MWOClient.exe ; for prefix: ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/342200/pfx ) If you want launch game from steam library without running lutris (like me, for use steam controller or another reasons), just create .destop file that contain this: Exec=env WINEPREFIX="/home/user/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/342200/pfx/" /home/user/.local/share/lutris/runners/wine/tkg-mwo-4.1-x86_64/bin/wine /home/user/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/"MechWarrior Online"/Bin64/MWOClient.exe . Better if you create file in game folder - simpliest way for fix path in steam (on me steam missing all after spaces in launcher ways). Also i recomend instal latest DXVK (1.0 release now)
title: MechWarrior Online™ Solaris 7

notes: So as the previous user had described. I downloaded the TKG-MWO runner from lutris and set up that .desktop file. Everything ran pretty good on DX11 but I'd recommend turning it to DX9 in game. I was able to go to max settings on DX9 with a solid 60FPS with minor hiccups here and there. Otherwise, this runs great!
title: MechWarrior Online™ Solaris 7

notes: Game launches just like in previous versions of Proton, but doesn't get past the login screen without several dozen attempts.  Even then, it will eventually crash with an Unknown Error message popping up just before the game hard closes.
title: MechWarrior Online™ Solaris 7

notes: Disabling esync and clearing Shaders and UIWebDownloads gets to the login, but it crashes after logging in while loading the mechlab.

notes: As described. Downloaded TKG-MWO runner from lutris and set up the .desktop file. Default settings were low frame rate on a Quadro P600, I'll have to make some adjustments.

notes: ProtonDB is for Proton, not Lutris.  And under Proton it is borked.
title: MechWarrior Online™ Solaris 7

notes: Crashes immediately on Proton. Runs with Lutris method, but performance is a lot worse than WIndows and there are graphical glitches.
title: MechWarrior Online™ Solaris 7

notes: Does not even get to menu screen before crashing with "Unexpected Error" - Can get rid of unsupported graphics card message by supplying "... %command% -noprompt -anygpu". Tried with PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 and PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1
title: MechWarrior Online™ Solaris 7

notes:
Had to compile proton from the Valve git with a 1-line patch for wine, to replace the 'static ULONG get_full_path_helper' function (total time to obtain and compile around an hour). Only weird requirements for compilation are vagrant, docker and virtualbox.
Instructions here: https://mwomercs.com/forums/topic/268847-running-the-game-on-ubuntu-steam-play/page__view__findpost__p__6235120 .
This results in  Proton_4.2-local showing as a "Steam Play Compatibiliy tool".
Played flawlessly for about an hour now.
Without this patch, there will be random crashes based on heap usage.
title: MechWarrior Online™ Solaris 7

notes: only works with lutris and the mwo runner - which is NOT proton.
title: MechWarrior Online™ Solaris 7

cpu: AMD Ryzen 5 3500U with Radeon Vega Mobile Gfx
notes: Running debian unstable branch distro siduction. Installed game via steam and enabled proton. Then cloned latest lutris from github source. In lutris did a bit of optimisation to get it to work. Installed tkg mwo 4.1 wine version got major framerate improvements using DXVK for DX11 and eesync. Plays in mid 30fps on what is essentially a business Lenovo ryzen laptop. (note: without DXVK and Eesync and dual channel RAM graphics framerate is barely 20fps ).
title: MechWarrior Online™ Solaris 7

notes: Doesn't start at all, just a black window with orange cursor.
title: MechWarrior Online™ Solaris 7

notes: Game seems to work well at 4k on my 1080TI (50fps at high settings). HOWEVER you need to enable proton-fixes or run proton-GE which has them enabled. Other then that it seems fine. Maybe we can get proton-fixes mainlined someday but until then you need them.
title: MechWarrior Online™ Solaris 7
